laws.title: Public Law 9-52 | The Federal District Wages Act of 2022
laws.final_statutory_text: Source unavailable; no operative statutory text can be verified.
laws.chronology: Source-limited. The public-law record is unrecoverable, so no later-law or repeal chronology can be lawfully pinned down from the text itself.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No determinable repeal, transfer, or redesignation chain could be resolved from the available source.
laws.status_basis: The source text is unrecoverable, so the law cannot be classified beyond a historical note without invention.

current_implementation.detail: A default labor/wage anchor was chosen despite no recoverable statutory text. The current note should be treated as source-limited history only.

recommended_actions: Retain only a concise source-limited historical note.
Remove the default Title 29 placement if no operative text can be recovered.

laws.title: Public Law 9-53 | Save Our Businesses Act of 2022
laws.final_statutory_text: Section 5 expands 3 U.S.C. 301 to authorize executive-branch delegation, creation of departments or agencies, transfer or suspension of statutory authority and personnel, Capitol Police authority/personnel changes with written congressional consent, and training or certification requirements.
laws.chronology: The operative 3 U.S.C. 301 amendment is live in the current branch, but later enactment PL 12-84 partially strikes sections 5-7 of this act, so the law is not cleanly treated as wholly current.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Section 5 is a real textual amendment to 3 U.S.C. 301; later-law review shows partial displacement of the act's later sections rather than a clean wholesale repeal of the section 301 amendment.
laws.status_basis: The act contains permanent, generally applicable amendments, and the section 301 change remains operative, but later enactment PL 12-84 cuts back sections 5-7 of the act.

current_implementation.detail: The operative 3 U.S.C. 301 text is integrated, but the note still carries a full source dump. Later-law effects on sections 5-7 are not reflected in the note treatment.

recommended_actions: Keep the 3 U.S.C. 301 amendment as operative text.
Trim the note to a concise amendment-history entry and account for the later partial strike of sections 5-7.

laws.title: Public Law 9-54 | The Smart Label Congress Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Source unavailable; no operative statutory text can be verified.
laws.chronology: Source-limited. The archive does not provide recoverable statutory text, so no lawful chronology beyond note-only treatment can be fixed from the source itself.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No determinable repeal, transfer, or redesignation chain could be resolved from the available source.
laws.status_basis: The source text is unrecoverable, so the law remains a note-only historical record without inventing operative text.

current_implementation.detail: The current note is a reasonable history-only treatment at the closest subject anchor, and no operative language was invented.

recommended_actions: Keep as a concise historical note only.
Do not invent operative text or a new code section.

laws.title: Public Law 9-55 | Economic Sanctions on Iran Act of 2022
laws.final_statutory_text: Historical sanctions/foreign-relations enactment only; no live operative text remains after repeal.
laws.chronology: Fully repealed or superseded in the archive; the act is not treated as current positive law.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Complete repeal with no surviving transfer or redesignation requiring operative text.
laws.status_basis: The law is recorded as repealed and does not require positive-law codification.

current_implementation.detail: The note sits at the correct foreign-relations subject area, but it still carries an unnecessary full-text dump and Trello record.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Keep only a concise historical note in Title 22.

laws.title: Public Law 9-56 | Federal Judicial Reform Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Federal judiciary restructuring text: short title, definitions, Supreme Court changes, new District Court structure, magistrates, savings, and immediate effective date.
laws.chronology: Later-law review did not identify a clean surviving positive-law placement in the current branch; the enactment is treated as repealed and historical in the present archive.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No surviving transfer or redesignation was resolved; the act is treated as a historical repealed judiciary-reform enactment.
laws.status_basis: The judiciary-reform enactment is archived as repealed and should remain history only.

current_implementation.detail: The retained note is anchored in Title 50, which is the wrong subject for a judiciary bill. The current dump and Trello link should be removed.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Move any retained history note out of Title 50 and into the Title 28 judiciary area.

laws.title: Public Law 9-57 | Repealing S.B.1/Pub. Law 7-39
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals S.B.1 / Public Law 7-39 and restores the prior law that it displaced.
laws.chronology: The repeal effect is self-executing and still meaningful as statutory history; no later express repeal of this repealer was identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: The law is a repeal-only enactment that operates on PL 7-39; no new permanent code text is needed for the repealer itself.
laws.status_basis: This is a spent repealer whose operative effect on PL 7-39 stands; it is not a positive-law section to be codified as standalone text.

current_implementation.detail: The note is parked in Title 54, which is the wrong subject for a FLETC repeal/history entry. It should be tied to the PL 7-39 anchor instead.

recommended_actions: Move the note off Title 54 and attach it to the PL 7-39 anchor.
Keep the repeal history only; no operative text section is needed.

laws.title: Public Law 9-58 | To Bar PhillipRobecq From Public Office
laws.final_statutory_text: Private/individual barment measure targeting one named person; no permanent general Code text is appropriate.
laws.chronology: Fully repealed or superseded and not suitable for positive-law codification in any event.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No transfer or redesignation issue; the measure is inherently individual and excluded from the permanent Code.
laws.status_basis: This is a private, person-specific measure and should remain history only after repeal.

current_implementation.detail: The retained note is placed in an unrelated shipping title, which is the wrong subject for an individual barment measure.

recommended_actions: Exclude from permanent Code.
Keep at most a concise historical note with no operative codification.

laws.title: Public Law 9-59 | M.P.D Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Local MPD restructuring text that was later expressly repealed by PL 9-60.
laws.chronology: Fully repealed by PL 9-60; no later surviving operative text remains for the original M.P.D. Act.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Complete repeal by a later law in the same cluster; no surviving transfer or redesignation requires operative text.
laws.status_basis: Expressly repealed by PL 9-60, so the law now belongs in history only.

current_implementation.detail: The note is in the right subject area for D.C. policing, but it should be reduced to a concise repeal-history entry because the act is no longer operative.

recommended_actions: Keep a concise repeal-history note only.
Remove any remaining noisy or duplicative note material.

laws.title: Public Law 9-60 | The Federal Metro Policing Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Federal Metro policing scheme that repealed PL 9-59 and reorganized MPD under DOJ control.
laws.chronology: Later enactment PL 12-84 expressly repeals S.B. 20, so this act is fully superseded in the current corpus.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: It repeals PL 9-59 and later itself gets repealed; no surviving standalone code section remains appropriate.
laws.status_basis: The act is expressly repealed by later law PL 12-84 after first repealing PL 9-59.

current_implementation.detail: The retained note is in the wrong subject area and still carries a full-text dump plus Trello link. It should be reduced to a concise repeal/history note under the MPD anchor.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Keep only a concise repeal-history note tied to the MPD family of provisions.

laws.title: Public Law 9-61 | Charter Repeal Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals the Washington, District of Columbia City Charter.
laws.chronology: Later enactment PL 12-84 expressly repeals H.R. 7, so the charter-repeal act is itself fully superseded in the current corpus.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Complete repeal with no surviving transfer or redesignation; the proper treatment is historical note only.
laws.status_basis: The act is later expressly repealed by PL 12-84 and remains only as a repeal-history record.

current_implementation.detail: The note is in the correct D.C.-charter subject area, but it still carries a source dump and should be trimmed to a concise repeal-history entry.

recommended_actions: Trim the note to a concise repeal-history statement.
Remove the quoted source dump and keep the D.C. charter repeal as history only.
